name: Amazon DynamoDB
description: Fully managed, serverless NoSQL database with single-digit millisecond performance at any scale

hosting-options.comment: DynamoDB is only available as an AWS managed service; no self-hosted option exists

primary-language.comment: Internal AWS implementation, language not disclosed

data-model.comment: Supports both key-value and document data models

topology.comment: Uses leaderless architecture with consistent hashing; based on the original Dynamo paper

sharding.comment: Automatic partitioning based on partition key using consistent hashing

replication-factor.comment: Data automatically replicated across 3 Availability Zones within a region

multi-region.comment: Global Tables provide multi-active replication across AWS regions

cap-position.comment: Supports both eventually consistent and strongly consistent reads; defaults to AP but can be tuned to CP per-operation

consistency-model.comment: Configurable per-request: eventual consistency (default) or strong consistency

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions via TransactWriteItems and TransactGetItems APIs

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span up to 100 items across multiple tables within a single region; not cross-region

distributed-transactions.comment: Cross-partition transactions within a single region; uses optimistic concurrency control

query-language.comment: Primary access via API; PartiQL provides SQL-like syntax for queries

sql-compatibility.comment: PartiQL provides limited SQL-like syntax but no JOINs or complex SQL features

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports both Local Secondary Indexes (LSI) and Global Secondary Indexes (GSI)

joins.comment: No JOIN support; denormalization is recommended

aggregations.comment: No native aggregation support; must be done client-side or via Streams

full-text-search.comment: No built-in full-text search; integration with OpenSearch recommended

max-cluster-size.comment: Serverless architecture with no exposed node concept; scales automatically without explicit cluster sizing

write-throughput.comment: Handles peak traffic over 500,000 requests per second; processes over 1 billion requests per hour for Amazon properties

read-latency-p99.comment: Consistent single-digit millisecond latency at any scale; microseconds with DAX caching

auto-scaling.comment: On-demand mode provides automatic scaling with no capacity planning; provisioned mode also supports auto-scaling policies

hot-spot-handling.comment: Adaptive capacity automatically isolates hot partitions; partition splitting for sustained load

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Fully managed service with zero downtime maintenance

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-less design; GSI can be added online

point-in-time-recovery.comment: PITR enables restore to any second within the last 35 days

backup-strategy.comment: On-demand backups (snapshots) and continuous backups with PITR

kubernetes-operator.comment: No Kubernetes operator as DynamoDB is only available as a managed service

rust-driver.comment: Official AWS SDK for Rust includes DynamoDB support

go-driver.comment: Official AWS SDK for Go v2

java-driver.comment: Official AWS SDK for Java 2.x with Enhanced Client

orm-support.comment: No traditional ORM support due to NoSQL nature; SDKs provide high-level abstractions like DynamoDB Enhanced Client

open-source.comment: Proprietary AWS service; not open source

free-tier.comment: 25GB storage, 25 WCUs, 25 RCUs free per month

pricing-model.comment: On-demand: per-request pricing; Provisioned: per-hour for capacity units; both charge per-GB for storage

cost-estimate-small.comment: Highly variable based on usage pattern; 25GB storage free, $0.625/million writes, $0.125/million reads (Nov 2024 pricing)

github-stars.comment: Proprietary service with no public repository

change-data-capture.comment: DynamoDB Streams provides time-ordered sequence of item-level changes; also integrates with Kinesis Data Streams

watch-subscribe.comment: No direct client push subscriptions; changes must be consumed via DynamoDB Streams or Kinesis with Lambda/polling

triggers.comment: Lambda triggers automatically execute on item-level changes via DynamoDB Streams; supports batch processing and parallelization

event-streaming.comment: Native Kinesis Data Streams integration; DynamoDB Streams is a proprietary CDC mechanism. Kafka available via third-party connectors (Confluent, MSK Connect) but not native
